_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of general attack stages (e.g., foot printing and scanning, enumeration, gaining access, escalation or privileges, maintaining access, network exploitation, covering tracks).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encryption at rest and in transit, identity and access management, and audit logging.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-005 (RM, cosine: 0.582), SP-K-012 (SP, cosine: 0.526).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.1/10 (raw 0.410). Shared vocabulary: [access, knowledge, network]. DCWF-only terms [attack, covering, enumeration, escalation, exploitation, foot] vs WIDAI-only terms [audit, controls, data, encryption, identity, isolation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.864 (P=0.857, R=0.870). Balanced P/R — the 22-word DCWF element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of general attack stages (e.g., foot printing and scanning, enumeratio" and "Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encry". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.587 > STS 0.410 (gap +0.177). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [access, knowledge, network] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.5%. Shared tokens: [access, knowledge, network]. DCWF-only: [attack, covering, enumeration, escalation, exploitation, foot]. WIDAI-only: [audit, controls, data, encryption, identity, isolation].
